overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete may be void under Cal. B&P Code §16600, which prohibits restraints on lawful trade or business.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may be free to operate a competing wireless business immediately after termination or expiration, regardless of the 2-year/10-mile restriction in the agreement.

overrides.override_summary: Indiana forum selection and Indiana choice of law may not be enforceable under California law. Franchisees encouraged to consult counsel re: Cal. B&P §20040.5, CCP §1281, Federal Arbitration Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may be able to litigate or arbitrate disputes in California rather than Indiana.

overrides.override_summary: Cal. Corp. Code §31512 voids waiver of FIL rights. Cal. B&P §20010 voids waiver of FRA rights. No questionnaire/acknowledgement may waive claims or disclaim reliance.
overrides.why_it_matters: Required general releases at renewal/transfer cannot waive California franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Certain liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under Cal. Civ. Code §1671.
overrides.why_it_matters: The $100,000 non-compete liquidated damages and $1,000/day IP infringement penalties may be challenged.

overrides.override_summary: No release language will relieve franchisor from liability imposed by Hawaii franchising laws.
overrides.why_it_matters: General release at renewal/transfer cannot waive Hawaii franchise law protections.

overrides.override_summary: Section 19 of Franchise Agreement (likely confidentiality/non-disparagement) deleted entirely and replaced with 'INTENTIONALLY OMITTED'.
overrides.why_it_matters: Hawaii franchisees have reduced post-term confidentiality and potentially non-compete obligations.

overrides.override_summary: Post-term non-compete limited per IC 23-2-2.7-1(9): cannot prohibit competition for more than 3 years or in area greater than exclusive area.
overrides.why_it_matters: The 2-year/10-mile post-term restriction is within Indiana's limits, but the 10-mile radius (which may extend far beyond the 2-mile Protected Territory) could be challenged.

overrides.override_summary: Choice of forum in any jurisdiction other than Indiana prohibited per IC 23-2-2.7-1(10). Franchisor may not require alternative dispute resolution other than arbitration before an independent arbitrator.
overrides.why_it_matters: For Indiana franchisees, the neutral-location mediation requirement may be overridden — disputes would stay in Indiana.

overrides.override_summary: IC 23-2-2.7-1(5) deems it unlawful to prospectively assent to a release that purports to relieve franchisor from liability imposed by Indiana franchise law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Required general releases at renewal/transfer cannot waive Indiana franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Provision requiring arbitration or litigation outside Michigan is void (though franchisee may agree at time of arbitration to out-of-state location).
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchisees can insist on in-state dispute resolution.

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause (failure to comply with lawful provisions + 30-day cure). Refusal to renew requires fair compensation by repurchase or FMV for inventory/supplies/equipment/fixtures (conditions apply).
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan provides additional termination protections and compensation rights beyond the franchise agreement.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must comply with Minn. Stat. §80C.14 subd. 3-5 for termination and non-renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota requires good cause, 90-day notice, and 60-day cure period for termination, which may override shorter cure periods in the agreement.

overrides.override_summary: Detailed supplemental disclosures per Article 33 GBL. No pending administrative/criminal/civil actions. No prior convictions. No injunctive orders.
overrides.why_it_matters: New York provides additional transparency protections for franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Covenants not to compete considered generally unreasonable per NDCC 51-19-09.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees may effectively have no enforceable post-term non-compete.

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law provision may not be enforceable if inconsistent with North Dakota law.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees may be able to apply North Dakota law rather than Indiana law.

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire/acknowledgement may waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements.
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's right to pursue fraud-in-the-inducement claims.

overrides.override_summary: RI Franchise Investment Act §19-28.1-14: provision restricting jurisdiction/venue outside state or requiring other state's law is void for claims under the Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: Rhode Island franchisees can bring franchise law claims in Rhode Island courts under Rhode Island law.

overrides.override_summary: VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564: unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause. Termination grounds not constituting 'reasonable cause' may be unenforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia franchisees have additional termination protections.

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire/acknowledgement may waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance.
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's right to pursue fraud-in-the-inducement claims.

overrides.override_summary: In disputes involving WA franchise, arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed location. Litigation may be brought in WA for franchise law claims.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchisees do not have to travel to Indiana for dispute resolution.

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver of WA Franchise Investment Protection Act rights is void except in negotiated settlement after agreement in effect with independent counsel (RCW 19.100.220(2)).
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard general releases at renewal/transfer cannot waive Washington franchise law rights.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate under any grounds permitted by state law. Buy-back provisions without franchisee consent unlawful during term unless for good cause (RCW 19.100.180(2)(j)). Fair and reasonable pricing required for required purchases.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington limits franchisor's buyout rights and provides franchisee termination rights not in the agreement.

overrides.override_summary: In event of conflict, WA Franchise Investment Protection Act (ch. 19.100 RCW) prevails over franchise agreement.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchise law supersedes conflicting contract terms.

overrides.override_summary: Wisconsin Fair Dealership Law grants 90-day prior written notice of termination and 60 days to cure. Franchise Agreement termination provisions superseded to extent inconsistent.
overrides.why_it_matters: Wisconsin franchisees get significantly more time (90-day notice + 60-day cure) than the 30-day cure in the agreement.

overrides.override_summary: If franchisor repurchases inventory sold to franchisee for resale, fair wholesale market value must be paid per §135.045.
overrides.why_it_matters: Wisconsin protects franchisee's investment in inventory at termination.
